title: Docker compatibility: Timestamps are a different format than docker
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Start a container, pull an image, or do anything else that results in the docker API returning a timestamp

**Describe the results you received:**
All timestamps (such as `StartedAt` and `CreatedAt`) are longer in podman than docker.
"StartedAt":"2021-05-07T13:28:21.783776413-04:00" <- podman
"StartedAt":"2021-05-07T17:27:52.347500403Z" <- docker

Clients, such as `docker-java`, cannot parse the longer timestamps resulting in exceptions, like this:
```
Caused by: java.time.format.DateTimeParseException: Text '2021-05-07T14:37:12.414551043-04:00' could not be parsed at index 29
	at java.base/java.time.format.DateTimeFormatter.parseResolved0(DateTimeFormatter.java:2046)
	at java.base/java.time.format.DateTimeFormatter.parse(DateTimeFormatter.java:1948)
	at org.testcontainers.utility.DockerStatus.isDockerTimestampNonEmpty(DockerStatus.java:78)
	at org.testcontainers.utility.DockerStatus.isContainerStopped(DockerStatus.java:67)
```

Here's an example from docker:
```javascript
{"Id":"sha256:f80194ae2e0ccf0f098baa6b981396dfbffb16e6476164af72158577a7de2dd9","RepoTags":["alpine:3.5"],"RepoDigests":["alpine@sha256:66952b313e51c3bd1987d7c4ddf5dba9bc0fb6e524eed2448fa660246b3e76ec"],"Parent":"","Comment":"","Created":"2019-01-30T22:20:40.179652676Z","Container":"3dcfc9c1f7f8d3ef3a808cc9c492e13d966771908d1e75dca22bdaec5e750bc0","ContainerConfig":{"Hostname":"3dcfc9c1f7f8","Domainname":"","User":"","AttachStdin":false,"AttachStdout":false,"AttachStderr":false,"Tty":false,"OpenStdin":false,"StdinOnce":false,"Env":["PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin"],"Cmd":["/bin/sh","-c","#(nop) ","CMD [\\"/bin/sh\\"]"],"ArgsEscaped":true,"Image":"sha256:be4705c10dd809a3a3b0dfd96016943fa43b8731e17321be9e8fe32a7426da62","Volumes":null,"WorkingDir":"","Entrypoint":null,"OnBuild":null,"Labels":{}},"DockerVersion":"18.06.1-ce","Author":"","Config":{"Hostname":"","Domainname":"","User":"","AttachStdin":false,"AttachStdout":false,"AttachStderr":false,"Tty":false,"OpenStdin":false,"StdinOnce":false,"Env":["PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin"],"Cmd":["/bin/sh"],"ArgsEscaped":true,"Image":"sha256:be4705c10dd809a3a3b0dfd96016943fa43b8731e17321be9e8fe32a7426da62","Volumes":null,"WorkingDir":"","Entrypoint":null,"OnBuild":null,"Labels":null},"Architecture":"amd64","Os":"linux","Size":3995534,"VirtualSize":3995534,"GraphDriver":{"Data":{"MergedDir":"/var/lib/docker/overlay2/569b37dff748becb02407bc50dd54f17265f067b44cbabfe795d93b2ab164a7b/merged","UpperDir":"/var/lib/docker/overlay2/569b37dff748becb02407bc50dd54f17265f067b44cbabfe795d93b2ab164a7b/diff","WorkDir":"/var/lib/docker/overlay2/569b37dff748becb02407bc50dd54f17265f067b44cbabfe795d93b2ab164a7b/work"},"Name":"overlay2"},"RootFS":{"Type":"layers","Layers":["sha256:f566c57e6f2da2364c14195c832b922fd8f4813fd139b8fe45e3454c16e33975"]},"Metadata":{"LastTagTime":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}}
```

And from podman:
```javascript
{"Id":"sha256:f80194ae2e0ccf0f098baa6b981396dfbffb16e6476164af72158577a7de2dd9","RepoTags":["docker.io/library/alpine:3.5"],"RepoDigests":["docker.io/library/alpine@sha256:66952b313e51c3bd1987d7c4ddf5dba9bc0fb6e524eed2448fa660246b3e76ec","docker.io/library/alpine@sha256:f7d2b5725685826823bc6b154c0de02832e5e6daf7dc25a00ab00f1158fabfc8"],"Parent":"","Comment":"","Created":"2019-01-30T22:20:40.179652676Z","Container":"","ContainerConfig":null,"DockerVersion":"18.06.1-ce","Author":"","Config":{"Hostname":"","Domainname":"","User":"","AttachStdin":false,"AttachStdout":false,"AttachStderr":false,"Tty":false,"OpenStdin":false,"StdinOnce":false,"Env":["PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin"],"Cmd":["/bin/sh"],"Image":"","Volumes":null,"WorkingDir":"","Entrypoint":null,"OnBuild":null,"Labels":null},"Architecture":"amd64","Os":"linux","OsVersion":"18.06.1-ce","Size":4238777,"VirtualSize":4238777,"GraphDriver":{"Data":{"UpperDir":"/home/candrews/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/f566c57e6f2da2364c14195c832b922fd8f4813fd139b8fe45e3454c16e33975/diff","WorkDir":"/home/candrews/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/f566c57e6f2da2364c14195c832b922fd8f4813fd139b8fe45e3454c16e33975/work"},"Name":"overlay"},"RootFS":{"Type":"layers","Layers":["sha256:f566c57e6f2da2364c14195c832b922fd8f4813fd139b8fe45e3454c16e33975"]},"Metadata":{"LastTagTime":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}}
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Podman should use the same timestamp format to be compatible with docker.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 3.2.0-rc1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

comments.body: So in other words, timestamps in manifest must be UTC time, not local times?

> So in other words, timestamps in manifest must be UTC time, not local times?

To match docker, yes.

> Here's an example from docker:
> 
> ```js
> {"Id":"sha256:f80194ae2e0ccf0f098baa6b981396dfbffb16e6476164af72158577a7de2dd9","RepoTags":["alpine:3.5"],"RepoDigests":["alpine@sha256:66952b313e51c3bd1987d7c4ddf5dba9bc0fb6e524eed2448fa660246b3e76ec"],"Parent":"","Comment":"","Created":"2019-01-30T22:20:40.179652676Z","Container":"3dcfc9c1f7f8d3ef3a808cc9c492e13d966771908d1e75dca22bdaec5e750bc0","ContainerConfig":{"Hostname":"3dcfc9c1f7f8","Domainname":"","User":"","AttachStdin":false,"AttachStdout":false,"AttachStderr":false,"Tty":false,"OpenStdin":false,"StdinOnce":false,"Env":["PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin"],"Cmd":["/bin/sh","-c","#(nop) ","CMD [\\"/bin/sh\\"]"],"ArgsEscaped":true,"Image":"sha256:be4705c10dd809a3a3b0dfd96016943fa43b8731e17321be9e8fe32a7426da62","Volumes":null,"WorkingDir":"","Entrypoint":null,"OnBuild":null,"Labels":{}},"DockerVersion":"18.06.1-ce","Author":"","Config":{"Hostname":"","Domainname":"","User":"","AttachStdin":false,"AttachStdout":false,"AttachStderr":false,"Tty":false,"OpenStdin":false,"StdinOnce":false,"Env":["PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin"],"Cmd":["/bin/sh"],"ArgsEscaped":true,"Image":"sha256:be4705c10dd809a3a3b0dfd96016943fa43b8731e17321be9e8fe32a7426da62","Volumes":null,"WorkingDir":"","Entrypoint":null,"OnBuild":null,"Labels":null},"Architecture":"amd64","Os":"linux","Size":3995534,"VirtualSize":3995534,"GraphDriver":{"Data":{"MergedDir":"/var/lib/docker/overlay2/569b37dff748becb02407bc50dd54f17265f067b44cbabfe795d93b2ab164a7b/merged","UpperDir":"/var/lib/docker/overlay2/569b37dff748becb02407bc50dd54f17265f067b44cbabfe795d93b2ab164a7b/diff","WorkDir":"/var/lib/docker/overlay2/569b37dff748becb02407bc50dd54f17265f067b44cbabfe795d93b2ab164a7b/work"},"Name":"overlay2"},"RootFS":{"Type":"layers","Layers":["sha256:f566c57e6f2da2364c14195c832b922fd8f4813fd139b8fe45e3454c16e33975"]},"Metadata":{"LastTagTime":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}}
> ```
> 
> And from podman:
> 
> ```js
> {"Id":"sha256:f80194ae2e0ccf0f098baa6b981396dfbffb16e6476164af72158577a7de2dd9","RepoTags":["docker.io/library/alpine:3.5"],"RepoDigests":["docker.io/library/alpine@sha256:66952b313e51c3bd1987d7c4ddf5dba9bc0fb6e524eed2448fa660246b3e76ec","docker.io/library/alpine@sha256:f7d2b5725685826823bc6b154c0de02832e5e6daf7dc25a00ab00f1158fabfc8"],"Parent":"","Comment":"","Created":"2019-01-30T22:20:40.179652676Z","Container":"","ContainerConfig":null,"DockerVersion":"18.06.1-ce","Author":"","Config":{"Hostname":"","Domainname":"","User":"","AttachStdin":false,"AttachStdout":false,"AttachStderr":false,"Tty":false,"OpenStdin":false,"StdinOnce":false,"Env":["PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin"],"Cmd":["/bin/sh"],"Image":"","Volumes":null,"WorkingDir":"","Entrypoint":null,"OnBuild":null,"Labels":null},"Architecture":"amd64","Os":"linux","OsVersion":"18.06.1-ce","Size":4238777,"VirtualSize":4238777,"GraphDriver":{"Data":{"UpperDir":"/home/candrews/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/f566c57e6f2da2364c14195c832b922fd8f4813fd139b8fe45e3454c16e33975/diff","WorkDir":"/home/candrews/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/f566c57e6f2da2364c14195c832b922fd8f4813fd139b8fe45e3454c16e33975/work"},"Name":"overlay"},"RootFS":{"Type":"layers","Layers":["sha256:f566c57e6f2da2364c14195c832b922fd8f4813fd139b8fe45e3454c16e33975"]},"Metadata":{"LastTagTime":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}}
> ```

Am I missing something here? The timestamps in both examples (Created and Metadata.LastTagTime) are identical. I tried to repro it and got the same result. Inspecting images built or pulled with podman gives UTC timestamps just like docker.

That said, inspecting a container with podman does return local time in the following fields:
- Created
- StartedAt
- FinishedAt
- io.kubernetes.cri-o.Created
I submitted a PR for this. Still I think that Java client should also accept TS with timezone. It uses `DateTimeFormatter.ISO_INSTANT` but IMHO it should use `DateTimeFormatter.ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME`.

> I submitted a PR for this. Still I think that Java client should also accept TS with timezone. It uses `DateTimeFormatter.ISO_INSTANT` but IMHO it should use `DateTimeFormatter.ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME`.

I think so too. Docker *prefers* to use UTC time when making images/containers, but it's happy to parse non-UTC times too. It will then pass those on in its output. For example:
```
C:\Users\***>docker images
REPOSITORY                             TAG                 IMAGE ID            CREATED             SIZE
mcr.microsoft.com/windows/nanoserver   10.0.14393.1066     a943c29f0046        4 years ago         1.01GB

C:\Users\***>docker image inspect a943c29f0046
[
    {
        "Id": "sha256:a943c29f0046aa2764d32bf9dfeca1d816865729e3fa26be0259915bf78ac05e",
        "RepoTags": [
            "mcr.microsoft.com/windows/nanoserver:10.0.14393.1066"
        ],
        "RepoDigests": [
            "mcr.microsoft.com/windows/nanoserver@sha256:ded482e81f381c94458a1d12d995506ac331d6d3180ed6da24ba809b0849a46c"
        ],
        "Parent": "",
        "Comment": "",
        "Created": "2017-04-11T02:12:36.3606843-07:00",
        [...]
```

I concur here - there is no API guarantee around UTC formatting. Docker
upstream could change this any release without it being a breaking change.
This really seems like a bug in the Java library.

On Mon, May 10, 2021 at 07:24 Rob Cowsill ***@***.***> wrote:

> I submitted a PR for this. Still I think that Java client should also
> accept TS with timezone. It uses DateTimeFormatter.ISO_INSTANT but IMHO
> it should use DateTimeFormatter.ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME.
>
> I think so too. Docker *prefers* to use UTC time when making
> images/containers, but it's happy to parse non-UTC times too. It will then
> pass those on in its output. For example:
>
> C:\Users\***>docker images
> REPOSITORY                             TAG                 IMAGE ID            CREATED             SIZEmcr.microsoft.com/windows/nanoserver   10.0.14393.1066     a943c29f0046        4 years ago         1.01GB
>
> C:\Users\***>docker image inspect a943c29f0046
> [
>     {
>         "Id": "sha256:a943c29f0046aa2764d32bf9dfeca1d816865729e3fa26be0259915bf78ac05e",
>         "RepoTags": [
>             "mcr.microsoft.com/windows/nanoserver:10.0.14393.1066"
>         ],
>         "RepoDigests": [
>             ***@***.***:ded482e81f381c94458a1d12d995506ac331d6d3180ed6da24ba809b0849a46c"
>         ],
>         "Parent": "",
>         "Comment": "",
>         "Created": "2017-04-11T02:12:36.3606843-07:00",
>         [...]
>

I submitted a PR to testcontainers: https://github.com/testcontainers/testcontainers-java/pull/4073

Thank you for all of this information and for making the change to podman.
